description: Compute standard error rates and the 'pre-error speeding' effect for each candidate's SART data from ~/pebl/data/sart_data.csv (columns: participant_id, trial, stimulus, response, rt_ms), excluding the button-mashing artifact cand_99. For each participant, calculate the commission_error_rate (ratio of response 1 on stimulus 3 trials), omission_error_rate (ratio of response 0 on stimulus != 3 trials), and the speeding_effect_ms (pre_cw_rt_ms minus pre_ce_rt_ms). Pre-error RTs for a No-Go trial are defined as the mean RT of up to 3 immediately preceding valid Go trials (stimulus != 3, response 1, rt_ms > 0). Calculate pre_ce_rt_ms as the mean of these preceding RTs for all CE No-Go trials, and pre_cw_rt_ms for all CW No-Go trials (response 0 on stimulus 3). Produce a JSON report at ~/pebl/analysis/sart_report.json containing the participant metrics, the excluded candidate with reason, and the group_mean_speeding_effect_ms. The JSON must follow the schema: {"participants": [{"id", "commission_error_rate", "omission_error_rate", "pre_ce_rt_ms", "pre_cw_rt_ms", "speeding_effect_ms"}], "excluded": [{"id", "reason"}], "group_mean_speeding_effect_ms": value}.
